equations.name: Proton Motive Force
equations.cost_breakdown: With constant folding (2.303*kB/e treated as one literal coefficient c): mul(c, T) = 2n; mul(cT, DeltapH) = 2n; add_gen(Deltapsi, product) = 11n... but Deltapsi and cT*DeltapH are both positive real voltages, so add_pos applies: add_pos(Deltapsi, cT*DeltapH) = 3n. Total = 2+2+3 = 7n.
equations.cross_domain: PMF is a thermodynamic potential difference. Structurally analogous to Nernst equation (11n) but the pH correction term here is always positive, dropping add_gen to add_pos and saving 8n.

equations.name: Motor Torque
equations.cost_breakdown: mul(PMF, e) = 2n; mul(n_p, product) = 2n; numerator done at 4n. Denominator: if 2*pi is a precomputed constant, div(numerator, 2pi_const) = 2n. Total = 6n. If 2*pi computed explicitly: mul(2, pi) = 2n extra; total = 8n.
equations.cross_domain: Torque per proton channel times channel count. The div(mul_chain, 2pi) pattern also appears in angular momentum L=r*p/... and rotational power P=tau*omega.

equations.name: Proton Hop Rate (Arrhenius form)
equations.cost_breakdown: If activation ratio r = DeltaG/(kBT) is precomputed: DEML(0, r) = exp(-r) = 1n; mul(k0, DEML) = 2n; div for ratio = 2n. Total from ratio: 3n. Standalone: div(DeltaG, kBT) = 2n; DEML(0, ratio) = 1n; mul(k0, exp) = 2n. Total = 5n. Alternatively adding ln-domain: ln(k0) + (-DeltaG/kBT) then exp = 1+2+1=4n + mul... 5n is the canonical standalone count.
equations.cross_domain: EXACT isomorph of Arrhenius rate constant k=A*exp(-Ea/RT). Also identical to: radioactive decay rate lambda*exp(-lambda*t), RC circuit discharge, first-order chemical decay. All are the 5n exponential decay family. The motor proton hop rate is Arrhenius — the math does not know it is biology.

equations.name: Motor Switching Probability (Hill Equation)
equations.cost_breakdown: pow([CheY-P], H) = 3n; pow(Kd, H) = 3n; add_pos(Kd^H, [CheY-P]^H) = 3n (both positive); div([CheY-P]^H, denominator) = 2n. Total = 3+3+3+2 = 11n.
equations.cross_domain: Hill equation is exact isomorph of: Michaelis-Menten (H=1, costs 7n), Pythagorean expectation W=RS^e/(RS^e+RA^e) (11n), enzyme cooperativity, ligand-receptor binding. The 11n cost is the structural signature of the 'two-pow + divide' family. At H=1, one pow becomes trivial and cost drops to 7n.

equations.name: Motor Efficiency
equations.superbest_cost: 10n (full form); 4n (simplified, tau cancels with torque definition)
equations.cost_breakdown: Full form: mul(tau, omega) = 2n numerator; mul(e, rate) = 2n, mul(PMF, product) = 2n, mul(n_p, product) = 2n denominator = 6n; div(num, denom) = 2n. Total = 10n. Simplified form substituting tau = n_p*PMF*e/(2*pi): cancellation leaves eta = omega/(2*pi*rate). Treating 2*pi as constant: div(omega, mul(2pi_const, rate)) = 2n + 2n = 4n.
equations.cross_domain: Mechanical efficiency ratio. The simplified form omega/(2*pi*rate) is structurally a 4n division after constant absorption. The full 10n form is the general mul-chain-divide pattern shared with thermodynamic efficiency eta=W/Q_hot.
